# aws-github-oidc-scoped-role

> OpenTofu/Terraform pattern for GitHub Actions OIDC trust with AWS IAM. Covers the non-obvious `job_workflow_ref` condition (vs just `sub` for repo+branch), the Bedrock inference profile ARN patterns, required `aws-marketplace` permissions alongside Bedrock, and the ReadOnlyAccess + explicit Deny pattern for AI agent roles. Use when wiring GitHub Actions to AWS via OIDC.

## Install

### APM (recommended)

```bash
apm install mizchi/skills/aws/github-oidc-scoped-role
```

Or declare it in `apm.yml` for a reproducible setup:

```yaml
dependencies:
  apm:
    - mizchi/skills/aws/github-oidc-scoped-role
```

then run `apm install`.

### Any agentskills.io-compatible agent

This is an [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io) skill — a portable
`SKILL.md` folder that works with Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Gemini CLI,
Cursor, and other [compatible agents](https://agentskills.io/clients).

With the [`skills`](https://github.com/vercel-labs/skills) CLI (installs into
every detected agent's skills directory):

```bash
npx skills add mizchi/skills --skill aws-github-oidc-scoped-role
```

Or copy the folder in directly:

```bash
npx degit mizchi/skills/aws/github-oidc-scoped-role ~/.claude/skills/aws-github-oidc-scoped-role
```

See [SKILL.md](./SKILL.md) for what this skill does and when to use it.
